record.field:
Elastic Integration SOP

Purpose

This Knowledge section explains the end-to-end operating procedure for the Elastic Integration app. It covers SFTP connection setup, configuration, product and customer data preparation, feature import, flat-file generation, manual exports, order imports, scheduling, logging, and troubleshooting.

Process Map

  1. Configure Beta and Production SFTP connections.
  2. Select the active environment and file format settings.
  3. Prepare Odoo products, variants, customers, catalogs, reps, pricelists, and cross-references.
  4. Generate product metadata and optionally import product feature assignments from Shopify.
  5. Run manual exports and confirm generated files in the active connection outbound directory.
  6. Import Elastic order files from the active connection inbound directory.
  7. Activate and schedule imports and exports through Odoo scheduled actions.
  8. Monitor Export Logs, Import Logs, and Staged Orders after each run.

Child Pages

  • 01 - Connection Setup: SFTP profiles, authentication, host keys, directories, and environment switching.
  • 02 - Core Configuration and Data Prep: settings, toggles, product/customer fields, catalogs, pricelists, reps, and cross-references.
  • 03 - Product Metadata and Feature Import: color/size metadata, product tag mappings, Shopify feature imports, and feature assignments.
  • 04 - End File Generation and Manual Exports: generated CSV files, file names, data sources, filters, and export sequence.
  • 05 - Order Import and Staged Orders: inbound order polling, staging, duplicate handling, sale order creation, and retries.
  • 06 - Scheduling Imports and Exports: scheduled action activation, cadence, and operational controls.
  • 07 - Monitoring and Troubleshooting: logs, common failures, and recovery steps.

Roles

Role Responsibility
Elastic Manager Owns SFTP credentials, host-key verification, active environment selection, export/import settings, scheduled actions, metadata setup, and troubleshooting.
Elastic User Reviews configuration, runs permitted exports/imports, reviews logs, and manages staged orders as assigned.
Sales / Customer Operations Maintains customer SoldToID data, ship-to locations, sales reps, catalogs, price groups, order exceptions, and customer cross-references.
Product / Merchandising Maintains product identifiers, color/size metadata, catalogs, product tags, features, and Shopify feature mappings.
IT / Administrator Installs or upgrades the module, activates cron jobs, monitors technical errors, and coordinates access to Odoo and Elastic SFTP.

Navigation

The app adds an Elastic top-level menu in Odoo. Use:

  • Elastic > Configuration > Settings for active environment, export toggles, import settings, product metadata generation, and manual actions.
  • Elastic > Configuration > SFTP Connections for Beta and Production connection profiles.
  • Elastic > Configuration for catalogs, colors, sizes, features, product tag mappings, Shopify feature imports, customer cross-references, and Elastic pricelists.
  • Elastic > Orders > Staged Orders for imported Elastic orders that are pending, processed, duplicate, or in error.
  • Elastic > Logs for import and export logs.

01 - Connection Setup

Goal

Create and validate separate SFTP connection profiles for Elastic Beta and Elastic Production, then choose which environment the app should use for all imports and exports.

Prerequisites

  • Elastic SFTP host, port, username, and either password or SSH private key.
  • Elastic outbound, inbound, and archive directory paths.
  • Odoo access in the Elastic Manager group for credential and host-key fields.

Create a Beta Connection

  1. Go to Elastic > Configuration > SFTP Connections.
  2. Create a new connection named Elastic Beta.
  3. Set Environment to Beta / Sandbox.
  4. Enter SFTP host, port, and username.
  5. Select the authentication method: password or SSH key.
  6. Enter Export Directory, Import Directory, and Archive Directory.
  7. Use Fetch & Save Host Key when using verified host keys.
  8. Click Test Connection.

Create a Production Connection

  1. Create another connection named Elastic Production.
  2. Set Environment to Production.
  3. Enter the production credentials and directory paths.
  4. Fetch and save the host key, then test the connection.

Host-Key Policy

Use Verify Stored Host Key for normal operations. This pins the SFTP server key and protects the connection from server impersonation.

Trust on First Connect should only be temporary for troubleshooting. If any connection is still using that policy, the Settings page shows an advisory and the Upgrade Host Keys button.

Link Connections to the Active Configuration

  1. Go to Elastic > Configuration > Settings.
  2. Open the active Elastic Configuration.
  3. On SFTP Connections, select the Beta connection and Production connection.
  4. Choose Active Environment. Use Beta for testing and Production for live data.
  5. Click Test Active Connection.

Operational Rule

All manual exports, scheduled exports, manual imports, and scheduled imports use the active environment's connection. Switch the active environment deliberately and confirm the connection status before running live exports or imports.

02 - Core Configuration and Data Prep

Goal

Configure the data rules that determine which Odoo records are sent to Elastic and how Elastic order files are matched back to Odoo.

File Format Settings

  1. Go to Elastic > Configuration > Settings.
  2. Open Export Settings.
  3. Set Export File Delimiter to comma, pipe, or tab based on Elastic requirements.
  4. Set Export File Encoding. Use UTF-8 unless Elastic requests another encoding.
  5. Keep Include Header Row enabled unless Elastic explicitly requests headerless files.
  6. Set date and datetime formats used by configuration-driven fields.

Enable Export Types

On Export Settings, enable only the files that should be included when using Export All Enabled or the scheduled export job.

  • Products
  • Product Tags
  • Catalogs
  • Catalog Mappings
  • Features
  • Customers
  • Locations
  • Sales Reps
  • Rep Mappings
  • Inventory
  • Prices

Export Filters

Use Export Only Synced Products to limit product, price, inventory, feature, and tag exports to products with Elastic sync enabled. Product exports also require the template and variant to be enabled.

Use Export Only Synced Customers to limit customer, location, custom-field, and rep-mapping exports to customers with Elastic sync enabled.

Customer Setup

  1. Open the customer in Contacts.
  2. On the Elastic tab, confirm Push to Elastic.
  3. Enter Elastic Legacy Account # when Elastic SoldToID should use the legacy account number.
  4. Assign Customer Catalogs when catalog permission should be customer-specific.
  5. Assign Elastic Sales Rep when rep mapping is required.
  6. Set Elastic Price Level when customer export should send a price group other than the default.
  7. Set Drop Ship Approved when the custom fields export should send drop_ship = Y.

SoldToID Rule

If Use Legacy Account Number for SoldToID is enabled in Settings, the customer export and order matching use the customer's Elastic Legacy Account # when present. If it is blank, Odoo falls back to the Odoo contact ID.

Product and Variant Setup

  1. Open the product template.
  2. On the Elastic tab, confirm Push to Elastic.
  3. Enter Elastic ItemNumber for the style-level ItemNumber.
  4. Set template-level Product Permission Group, Available Date, and Catalogs when needed.
  5. Open each variant that needs overrides.
  6. Set variant-level Push to Elastic, Elastic SKU, Elastic ItemNumber Override, Elastic Stock Item Key, Product Permission Group, and Available Date as needed.

Catalog Setup

  1. Go to Elastic > Configuration > Catalogs.
  2. Create catalogs with unique codes.
  3. Add product templates, product variants, and customers as needed.
  4. Choose whether catalog mapping lines are generated by Odoo or loaded from an uploaded file.
  5. For generated mapping, set the sort method and click Generate Mapping Lines.
  6. For uploaded mapping, upload the CSV and click Load Mapping Upload.

Pricing Setup

Go to Elastic > Configuration > Pricelists (Elastic). Mark pricelists Send to Elastic and assign an Elastic Price Group Code. If no active pricelist is marked for Elastic, the price export falls back to product list price with price group LP.

Customer Cross-References

Use Elastic > Configuration > Customer Cross-References to maintain external Sold-To and Ship-To IDs when order files need explicit mapping to Odoo partners. During order import, mappings are also recorded automatically after a match is found.

03 - Product Metadata and Feature Import

Goal

Prepare Elastic color, size, tag, and feature metadata so product exports and feature exports contain governed values instead of ad hoc text.

Generate Color and Size Metadata

  1. Go to Elastic > Configuration > Settings.
  2. Open the Product Metadata tab.
  3. Click Generate Product Metadata.
  4. Review generated records under Colors, Size Scales, and Size Values.
  5. Clean up codes, color groups, sort orders, alternate sizes, and inactive records before exporting products.

Color metadata is seeded from product attribute values named Color, Colour, Frame Color, or Product Color. Size metadata is seeded from attributes named Size or ending in Size.

Maintain Attribute Value Overrides

Use Elastic > Configuration > Attribute Values or the Product Attribute form to maintain direct Elastic overrides on Odoo attribute values:

  • Elastic Color Code
  • Elastic Color
  • Elastic Color Name
  • Elastic Color Sort
  • Elastic Size Code
  • Elastic Size Name
  • Elastic Size Sort
  • Elastic Alternate Size

Product Tag Mappings

  1. Go to Elastic > Configuration > Product Tag Mappings.
  2. Create one mapping for each TagName Elastic should receive.
  3. Select whether the source field is on product template or product variant.
  4. Select the Odoo field that supplies TagValue.
  5. Choose whether values should be split by line, comma, semicolon, or not split.
  6. Run Export Product Tags or enable Product Tags in Export All.

The product tags export skips color attributes because color is already sent in products.csv.

Governed Feature Setup

  1. Go to Elastic > Configuration > Features.
  2. Create or review the target Elastic features.
  3. Set feature code, display order, type, filterable/searchable flags, and Export to Elastic.
  4. Maintain governed values under Feature Values when controlled values are required.
  5. Use Product Feature Assignments for manual or reviewed assignments.

Shopify Feature Import

  1. Go to Elastic > Configuration > Shopify Feature Imports.
  2. Create a Shopify connection with shop domain, API version, access token, and match strategy.
  3. Choose match strategy: Variant SKU, Variant Barcode, Shopify Product ID, or Shopify Handle.
  4. Enable Only Import Elastic Products when imports should skip products not enabled for Elastic.
  5. Add feature mappings from Shopify product fields or metafields to target Elastic features.
  6. Choose the parser: HTML list, HTML text, rich text, multiline, or plain text.
  7. Click Import Features.
  8. Review Product Feature Assignments.

Feature Export Rule

The features.csv export reads active Product Feature Assignments where the related feature is active and marked Export to Elastic. Template-level assignments are expanded to active variants. If Export Only Synced Products is enabled, unsynced templates or variants are skipped.
04 - End File Generation and Manual Exports

04 - End File Generation and Manual Exports

Goal

Generate the Elastic outbound files and upload them to the active SFTP connection's export directory.

File Generation Behavior

  • Files are generated with the delimiter, encoding, and header setting from Elastic > Configuration > Settings.
  • File names are stable and do not include timestamps.
  • Each successful export uploads to the active connection's Export Directory.
  • Each export creates an Elastic > Logs > Export Logs record with type, record count, file name, status, and message.

Generated Files

Button / Export File Primary Odoo Source
Export Customers customers.csv Company customers from Contacts
Export Custom Fields customer_custom_fields.csv Customer drop_ship approval and related custom fields
Export Locations locations.csv Customer primary addresses and delivery contacts
Export Products products.csv Sellable active product variants
Export Product Tags product_tags.csv Active product tag mappings applied to product/template fields
Export Features features.csv Active governed product feature assignments
Export Prices prices.csv Sellable active product variants and Elastic-enabled pricelists
Export Inventory inventory.csv Storable active products by active warehouse
Export Catalogs catalogs.csv Active Elastic catalogs
Export Mappings catalog_mapping.csv Catalog mapping lines generated by Odoo or loaded from upload
Export Reps reps.csv Active internal sales users
Export Rep Mappings rep_mappings.csv Customers assigned to an Elastic Sales Rep

Recommended Manual Export Sequence

  1. Test the active SFTP connection.
  2. Run Generate Product Metadata after product attributes are loaded or changed.
  3. Prepare catalogs and click Generate Mappings if using generated catalog mappings.
  4. Export catalogs.
  5. Export catalog mappings.
  6. Export products.
  7. Export product tags and features.
  8. Export prices.
  9. Export inventory.
  10. Export customers, custom fields, locations, reps, and rep mappings.
  11. Review Export Logs and confirm files appear in the Elastic outbound directory.

Run All Enabled

Use Export All Enabled when all required toggles are configured. This runs each enabled export, summarizes success and failure, and creates individual export logs for each file.

Validation Checklist

  • Products have ItemNumber and StockItemKey values.
  • Customers have SoldToID values that match Elastic expectations.
  • Pricelists marked Send to Elastic have unique price group codes.
  • Catalogs have unique codes and mapping lines.
  • Product tag mappings point to fields that still exist.
  • Feature assignments have values and active/exportable features.
  • Export logs show success and expected record counts.

05 - Order Import and Staged Orders

Goal

Poll Elastic order files from SFTP, stage each order/shipment group, and create Odoo draft sale orders with auditability and retry support.

Configure Order Import

  1. Go to Elastic > Configuration > Settings.
  2. Open Import Settings.
  3. Enable Order Import.
  4. Set Order File Pattern, such as *.csv or ORDER_*.csv.
  5. Choose whether to Auto-Confirm Orders. Leave disabled unless the business wants imported orders confirmed automatically.
  6. Choose whether to Archive Processed Files. Keep enabled for normal operation.
  7. Select Stock Item Key Field: UPC / Barcode, SKU, or Product Number + Variation Code + Size.

Manual Import

  1. Confirm the active SFTP environment is correct.
  2. Confirm order files are in the active connection's Import Directory.
  3. Click Import Orders Now.
  4. Review the notification summary.
  5. Open Elastic > Logs > Import Logs.
  6. Open Elastic > Orders > Staged Orders for pending, processed, duplicate, or error rows.

What the Importer Does

  1. Lists matching files in the active SFTP Import Directory.
  2. Downloads each matching file.
  3. Parses the file using the configured delimiter and encoding.
  4. Groups rows by Elastic Order Number and Shipment Number.
  5. Creates or updates one staged order per order/shipment group.
  6. Attempts to create one draft sale order per staged group.
  7. Marks duplicates when a sale order already exists for the same Elastic order and shipment keys.
  8. Writes errors to the staged order record for review.
  9. Moves processed source files to the active connection Archive Directory when archiving is enabled.
  10. Creates an Import Log with file count, order count, error count, status, and summary message.

Staged Order Review

Use Elastic > Orders > Staged Orders to review import outcomes.

  • Pending: staged but not processed yet.
  • Processed: sale order was created.
  • Duplicate: an existing sale order matched the Elastic order/shipment keys.
  • Error: processing failed and the error message needs review.

Retry Procedure

  1. Open the staged order in Error.
  2. Read the error message.
  3. Fix the cause, usually customer mapping, product matching, missing product, or invalid order data.
  4. Click Retry.
  5. If the retry creates a sale order, open it from the staged order.

Matching Notes

  • Sold-To and Ship-To matching uses customer cross-references first when available.
  • The customer legacy account number can be used as SoldToID when enabled in Settings.
  • Product matching follows the configured Stock Item Key Field.
  • Duplicate checks use the Elastic Order Number and Shipment Number stored on sale orders.

06 - Scheduling Imports and Exports

Goal

Activate Odoo scheduled actions so imports and exports run automatically against the active Elastic configuration.

Scheduled Actions Created by the Module

Scheduled Action Default Active Default Cadence Runs
Elastic: Import Orders No Every 1 hour Order import for active configurations where Enable Order Import is checked.
Elastic: Export All Enabled No Every 1 day All export types enabled on each active Elastic configuration.

Activate Scheduled Order Import

  1. Go to Settings > Technical > Automation > Scheduled Actions. Developer mode may be required.
  2. Open Elastic: Import Orders.
  3. Set the interval and next execution time according to the business schedule.
  4. Activate the scheduled action.
  5. Confirm Enable Order Import is checked on the active Elastic configuration.
  6. Confirm the active environment is correct.
  7. After the next run, review Import Logs and Staged Orders.

Activate Scheduled Export

  1. Open Elastic: Export All Enabled in Scheduled Actions.
  2. Set the interval and next execution time.
  3. Activate the scheduled action.
  4. In Elastic > Configuration > Settings, enable only the export types that should run automatically.
  5. Run Export All Enabled manually once before relying on the scheduled job.
  6. After the next run, review Export Logs and confirm files on SFTP.

Scheduling Controls

  • Scheduled exports run against active configurations and respect the export enable toggles.
  • Scheduled order imports run only for active configurations where order import is enabled.
  • Both scheduled actions use the active environment selected on each configuration.
  • Keep Beta scheduled actions disabled unless testing automation intentionally.
  • Do not activate Production schedules until manual Beta and Production test runs have been validated.

Recommended Production Cadence

  • Order import: every 15 to 60 minutes depending on order volume and service expectations.
  • Inventory export: at least daily, more often if Elastic needs near-current availability.
  • Prices, products, catalogs, features, and customers: daily or after relevant master-data changes.

07 - Monitoring and Troubleshooting

Daily Monitoring

  1. Open Elastic > Logs > Export Logs and review the most recent export run.
  2. Open Elastic > Logs > Import Logs and review the most recent import run.
  3. Open Elastic > Orders > Staged Orders and filter for Error or Pending records.
  4. Confirm expected files exist in the outbound directory and processed order files moved to archive.
  5. Escalate any failed logs or unprocessed staged orders before the next scheduled run.

Common Export Issues

Symptom Likely Cause Action
No records found Export toggle/filter is too restrictive, records are inactive, or required sync flags are off. Review export filters, active flags, Push to Elastic values, and record eligibility.
No valid records after transformation Required identifiers are missing, such as ItemNumber, StockItemKey, catalog code, or feature value. Review the specific export requirements and fill missing master data.
SFTP upload failed Connection, credential, host-key, or remote directory issue. Test the active connection, verify host key, confirm credentials, and confirm remote paths.
Unexpected price rows Pricelists marked Send to Elastic drive price groups; otherwise list price is used. Review Elastic pricelist settings and price group codes.

Common Import Issues

Symptom Likely Cause Action
No order files found File pattern does not match, files are in the wrong directory, or the active environment is wrong. Review Order File Pattern, active connection Import Directory, and Active Environment.
No Odoo customer found Missing Sold-To cross-reference or legacy account number mismatch. Create or correct the Customer Cross-Reference or customer Elastic Legacy Account #.
Product not found Order file product key does not match the configured Stock Item Key Field. Review UPC, SKU, or Product Number + Variation Code + Size data in Odoo and the file.
Duplicate staged order A sale order already exists for the Elastic order and shipment keys. Open the linked sale order and confirm no action is needed.
File did not archive Archive path is blank, archiving is disabled, or SFTP move failed. Review Archive Processed Files, Archive Directory, and SFTP permissions.

Recovery Rules

  • Fix master data first, then retry staged orders.
  • Use manual imports and exports to validate fixes before waiting for scheduled jobs.
  • Use Beta for integration testing whenever possible.
  • Keep failed log messages with the incident or support ticket.
  • Do not change the active environment during a scheduled run window.
